---
title: "flat_partytrees.Rmd empty"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 You need to run the 'description' chunk in the '0-dev_history.Rmd' file before continuing your code there.
-->



```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE)
```


# rank_wt
    
```{r development-rank_wt}
# You can prepare the code of the rank_wt() function here
```
  
```{r function-rank_wt}
#' Compute weighted fractional ranks
#'
#' Compute midpoint fractional ranks after sorting `x` in ascending order and
#' normalizing positive weights to sum to one.
#'
#' Observations with missing `x`, missing `wt`, or non-positive `wt` are
#' removed before ranking. The resulting ranks are useful as a low-level
#' building block for concentration-index calculations and related inequality
#' measures.
#'
#' @param x A numeric vector to rank.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of case weights with the same length as `x`.
#'
#' @return A numeric vector of weighted fractional ranks in `(0, 1)`, one for
#'   each retained observation. The output is ordered like the retained inputs
#'   after removing cases with missing `x`, missing `wt`, or `wt <= 0`.
#'
#' @export
rank_wt <- function(x, wt) {
  stopifnot(length(x) == length(wt))
  ok <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(wt) & wt > 0
  x <- x[ok]
  wt <- wt[ok]

  if (!length(x)) {
    return(numeric(0))
  }

  o <- order(x)
  x <- x[o]
  wt <- wt[o] / sum(wt)

  r <- numeric(length(x))
  if (length(x) == 1L) {
    r[1L] <- 0.5
  } else {
    r <- c(0, cumsum(wt[-length(wt)])) + wt / 2
  }

  out <- numeric(sum(ok))
  out[o] <- r
  out
}
```
  
```{r example-rank_wt}
rank_wt(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 1, 2))
rank_wt(c(1, 2, NA, 3), c(1, 0, 1, 2))
```
  
```{r tests-rank_wt}
test_that("rank_wt computes weighted midpoint ranks", {
  expect_equal(
    rank_wt(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 1, 2)),
    c(0.125, 0.375, 0.75)
  )
})

test_that("rank_wt returns ranks in the order of retained inputs", {
  expect_equal(
    rank_wt(c(20, 10, 30), c(1, 1, 2)),
    c(0.375, 0.125, 0.75)
  )
})

test_that("rank_wt drops missing and non-positive weights before ranking", {
  expect_equal(
    rank_wt(c(2, NA, 1, 3), c(1, 1, 0, 2)),
    c(1 / 6, 2 / 3)
  )
})

test_that("rank_wt handles edge cases", {
  expect_equal(rank_wt(5, 2), 0.5)
  expect_equal(rank_wt(c(1, 2), c(0, NA)), numeric(0))
  expect_error(rank_wt(1:2, 1))
})
```

```{r, function-ci_factory}
#' Create concentration-index scoring functions
#'
#' Build a function that computes a weighted concentration-index criterion from
#' a two-column response matrix. The first column is treated as the
#' socioeconomic ranking variable and the second as the health outcome.
#'
#' This factory is intended for inequality-aware split scoring, where different
#' concentration-index variants can be swapped in while keeping a common
#' `function(y, wt)` interface.
#'
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, or `"CIc"` selecting the standard,
#'   generalized, or corrected concentration index.
#'
#' @return A function with signature `function(y, wt)` where `y` is a two-column
#'   numeric matrix and `wt` is a vector of positive case weights. The returned
#'   function drops incomplete rows and non-positive weights, then returns a
#'   single non-negative concentration-index value. It returns `0` when fewer
#'   than two valid observations remain or when the required denominator is
#'   degenerate.
#'
#' @details The returned function first converts `y[, 1]` to weighted
#'   fractional ranks using [rank_wt()]. It then computes the weighted
#'   covariance between rank and outcome and maps it to the requested index:
#'   `"CI"` divides by the weighted mean of the outcome, `"CIg"` returns the
#'   generalized form, and `"CIc"` applies an Erreygers-style range correction.
#'   Absolute values are returned.
#' @export 

ci_factory <- function(type = c("CI", "CIg", "CIc")) {
  type <- match.arg(type)

  function(y, wt) {
    stopifnot(is.matrix(y), ncol(y) == 2L, length(wt) == nrow(y))

    ok <- stats::complete.cases(y) & !is.na(wt) & wt > 0
    y <- y[ok, , drop = FALSE]
    wt <- wt[ok]

    if (nrow(y) <= 1L) {
      return(0)
    }

    # column 1 = SES rank variable, column 2 = health outcome
    y[, 1] <- rank_wt(y[, 1], wt)

    cov12 <- stats::cov.wt(
      x = as.matrix(data.frame(y1 = y[, 1], y2 = y[, 2])),
      wt = wt
    )$cov[1, 2]

    if (type == "CI") {
      mu <- stats::weighted.mean(y[, 2], wt)
      if (isTRUE(all.equal(mu, 0))) {
        return(0)
      }
      return(abs(2 * cov12 / mu))
    }

    if (type == "CIg") {
      return(abs(2 * cov12))
    }

    # CIc = Erreygers-style corrected version
    rng <- max(y[, 2], na.rm = TRUE) - min(y[, 2], na.rm = TRUE)
    if (isTRUE(all.equal(rng, 0))) {
      return(0)
    }
    4 * abs(2 * cov12) / rng
  }
}
```

```{r, example-ci_factory)}
ci <- ci_factory("CI")
y <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
ci(y, c(1, 1, 2))
cig <- ci_factory("CIg")
cig(y, c(1, 1, 2))
cic <- ci_factory("CIc")
cic(y, c(1, 1, 2))
```

```{r, tests-ci_factory}
test_that("ci_factory returns scoring functions and validates type", {
  expect_true(is.function(ci_factory("CI")))
  expect_true(is.function(ci_factory("CIg")))
  expect_true(is.function(ci_factory("CIc")))
  expect_error(ci_factory("bad"))
})

test_that("ci_factory computes expected CI variants on a simple example", {
  y <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
  wt <- c(1, 1, 2)

  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  cig <- ci_factory("CIg")
  cic <- ci_factory("CIc")

  expect_equal(ci(y, wt), 2 / 15)
  expect_equal(cig(y, wt), 0.1)
  expect_equal(cic(y, wt), 0.4)
})

test_that("ci_factory drops incomplete and non-positive-weight rows", {
  y1 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30, NA), c(1, 0, 1, 1))
  wt1 <- c(1, 1, 2, 1)

  y2 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
  wt2 <- c(1, 1, 2)

  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  expect_equal(ci(y1, wt1), ci(y2, wt2))

  y3 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30, 40), c(1, 0, 1, 1))
  wt3 <- c(1, 1, 2, 0)
  expect_equal(ci(y3, wt3), ci(y2, wt2))
})

test_that("ci_factory returns zero for insufficient valid data", {
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  y <- cbind(c(10, NA), c(1, 0))
  wt <- c(1, 1)

  expect_equal(ci(y, wt), 0)
})

test_that("ci_factory handles degenerate denominators", {
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  cic <- ci_factory("CIc")

  y_mu0 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(0, 0, 0))
  expect_equal(ci(y_mu0, c(1, 1, 1)), 0)

  y_rng0 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(5, 5, 5))
  expect_equal(cic(y_rng0, c(1, 1, 1)), 0)
})

test_that("ci_factory is invariant to rescaling weights", {
  y <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")

  expect_equal(ci(y, c(1, 1, 2)), ci(y, c(10, 10, 20)))
})
```


```{r, function-weighted_ci_gain}
#' Compute weighted concentration-index gain for a binary split
#' It computes the gain in concentration index from splitting 
#'  a node into left and right parts, weighted by the case weights. 
#'  The gain is calculated as the difference between the parent node's
#'  concentration index and the weighted average of the left and right 
#'  child nodes' concentration indices.
#' @param y A matrix with two columns: SES rank variable and health outcome.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of weights.
#' @param left A logical vector indicating the left split.
#' @param ci_fun A function to compute the concentration index.
#' @return The weighted concentration-index gain.
#' @export
weighted_ci_gain <- function(y, wt, left, ci_fun) {
  stopifnot(length(left) == nrow(y), length(wt) == nrow(y))

  wl <- sum(wt[left], na.rm = TRUE)
  wr <- sum(wt[!left], na.rm = TRUE)
  wtot <- wl + wr

  if (wl <= 0 || wr <= 0 || wtot <= 0) return(-Inf)

  ci_parent <- ci_fun(y, wt)
  ci_left   <- ci_fun(y[left,  , drop = FALSE], wt[left])
  ci_right  <- ci_fun(y[!left, , drop = FALSE], wt[!left])

  ci_parent - ((wl / wtot) * ci_left + (wr / wtot) * ci_right)
}
```

```{r, example-weighted_ci_gain}
y <- cbind(
  ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1)
)
wt <- rep(1, 6)

# A candidate split evaluated outside any tree-fitting routine
left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)

weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, left, ci_factory("CI"))
```

```{r, tests-weighted_ci_gain}
test_that("weighted_ci_gain computes parent minus weighted child impurity", {
  y <- cbind(ses = 1:4, health = c(10, 20, 30, 40))
  wt <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)

  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, left, mock_ci_fun),
    110
  )
})

test_that("weighted_ci_gain returns -Inf for invalid splits", {
  y <- cbind(ses = 1:4, health = c(10, 20, 30, 40))
  wt <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)

  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE), mock_ci_fun),
    -Inf
  )
  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(
      y, c(0, 2, 3, 4),
      c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE), mock_ci_fun
    ),
    -Inf
  )
})

test_that("weighted_ci_gain validates input lengths", {
  y <- cbind(ses = 1:4, health = c(10, 20, 30, 40))
  wt <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)

  expect_error(weighted_ci_gain(
    y, wt,
    c(TRUE, FALSE), mock_ci_fun
  ))
  expect_error(weighted_ci_gain(
    y, 1:3,
    c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE), mock_ci_fun
  ))
})

test_that("weighted_ci_gain is invariant to global weight rescaling with CI", {
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1)
  )
  left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")

  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(y, rep(1, 6), left, ci),
    weighted_ci_gain(y, rep(10, 6), left, ci)
  )
})
```


```{r, function-best_numeric_split}
#' Find the best numeric split by concentration-index gain
#'
#' Evaluate all admissible cutpoints of a numeric predictor and return the
#' split with the largest weighted concentration-index gain.
#'
#' Candidate cutpoints are formed at midpoints between distinct sorted values of
#' `x`. Each candidate is filtered using `ctrl$minbucket` and `ctrl$minprob`
#' before being scored with [weighted_ci_gain()].
#'
#' @param x A numeric predictor vector.
#' @param y A two-column numeric matrix whose first column is the socioeconomic
#'   ranking variable and whose second column is the health outcome.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of case weights with the same length as `x`.
#' @param varid Integer identifier of the splitting variable, passed to
#'   [partykit::partysplit()].
#' @param ctrl A list-like control object containing `minbucket` and `minprob`.
#' @param ci_fun A concentration-index scoring function, typically created by
#'   [ci_factory()].
#'
#' @return A `partysplit` object describing the best admissible binary split, or
#'   `NULL` if no split is available because all `x` values are tied or all
#'   candidate splits violate the control constraints.
#'
#' @export
best_numeric_split <- function(
    x,
    y,
    wt,
    varid,
    ctrl,
    ci_fun) {
  ord <- order(x)
  x <- x[ord]
  y <- y[ord, , drop = FALSE]
  wt <- wt[ord]

  cut_pos <- which(diff(x) != 0)
  if (!length(cut_pos)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  gains <- rep(-Inf, length(cut_pos))

  for (k in seq_along(cut_pos)) {
    pos <- cut_pos[k]
    left <- seq_along(x) <= pos

    wl <- sum(wt[left], na.rm = TRUE)
    wr <- sum(wt[!left], na.rm = TRUE)
    wtot <- wl + wr

    if (wl < ctrl$minbucket || wr < ctrl$minbucket) next
    if ((wl / wtot) < ctrl$minprob || (wr / wtot) < ctrl$minprob) next

    gains[k] <- weighted_ci_gain(
      y = y,
      wt = wt,
      left = left,
      ci_fun = ci_fun
    )
  }

  if (!any(is.finite(gains))) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  best_k <- cut_pos[which.max(gains)]
  cut <- (x[best_k] + x[best_k + 1L]) / 2

  partykit::partysplit(
    varid = as.integer(varid),
    breaks = cut,
    index = 1:2
  )
}
```

```{r, example-best_numeric_split}
x <- c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10)
y <- cbind(
  ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
  health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1)
)
wt <- rep(1, 5)
ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

split <- best_numeric_split(
  x = x,
  y = y,
  wt = wt,
  varid = 1,
  ctrl = ctrl,
  ci_fun = ci_factory("CI")
)
split$breaks
```

```{r, tests-best_numeric_split}
test_that("best_numeric_split returns
 NULL when all x values are tied", {
  x <- c(5, 5, 5)
  y <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30), health = c(1, 0, 1))
  wt <- rep(1, 3)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

  expect_null(best_numeric_split(x, y, wt, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")))
})

test_that("best_numeric_split returns NULL
when no candidate split is admissible", {
  x <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  y <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40), health = c(1, 0, 1, 0))
  wt <- rep(1, 4)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 3, minprob = 0)

  expect_null(best_numeric_split(x, y, wt, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")))
})

test_that("best_numeric_split chooses the cutpoint with the largest gain", {
  x <- c(4, 1, 3, 2)
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(40, 10, 30, 20),
    health = c(10, 1, 3, 2)
  )
  wt <- rep(1, 4)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_numeric_split(x, y, wt,
    varid = 7, ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$varid, 7L)
  expect_equal(split$breaks, 3.5)
  expect_equal(split$index, 1:2)
})

test_that("best_numeric_split respects minprob when selecting splits", {
  x <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    health = c(1, 5, 10, 20, 40)
  )
  wt <- rep(1, 5)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0.4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_numeric_split(x, y, wt,
    varid = 1, ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_equal(split$breaks, 3.5)
})
```

```{r, function-best_factor_split}
#' Find the best factor split by concentration-index gain
#'
#' Evaluate cumulative binary splits of a factor predictor after ordering the
#' observed levels by weighted mean outcome, and return the split with the
#' largest weighted concentration-index gain.
#'
#' Only levels present among the kept observations are scored, but the returned
#' split keeps the full level mapping so it can be used with
#' [partykit::partysplit()].
#'
#' @param x_full A factor predictor containing all original levels.
#' @param keep A logical vector selecting the observations currently available
#'   for splitting.
#' @param y_full A two-column numeric matrix whose first column is the
#'   socioeconomic ranking variable and whose second column is the health
#'   outcome.
#' @param wt_full A numeric vector of case weights for all observations.
#' @param varid Integer identifier of the splitting variable, passed to
#'   [partykit::partysplit()].
#' @param ctrl A list-like control object containing `minbucket` and `minprob`.
#' @param ci_fun A concentration-index scoring function, typically created by
#'   [ci_factory()].
#'
#' @return A `partysplit` object describing the best admissible split of the
#'   observed factor levels, or `NULL` if fewer than two levels are present
#'   after subsetting or if all candidate splits violate the control
#'   constraints.
#' @export
best_factor_split <- function(
    x_full, keep, y_full, wt_full, varid, ctrl, ci_fun) {
  # keep full level mapping for partysplit()
  full_levels <- levels(x_full)
  x <- x_full[keep]
  y <- y_full[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  wt <- wt_full[keep]

  present_levels <- full_levels[full_levels %in% unique(as.character(x))]
  if (length(present_levels) <= 1L) return(NULL)

  # order factor levels by weighted mean outcome, then search cumulative
  # binary splits
  level_score <- sapply(
    present_levels,
    function(lv) {
      idx <- as.character(x) == lv
      stats::weighted.mean(y[idx, 2], wt[idx])
    }
  )

  ord_levels <- present_levels[order(level_score)]
  gains <- rep(-Inf, length(ord_levels) - 1L)

  for (k in seq_len(length(ord_levels) - 1L)) {
    left_levels <- ord_levels[seq_len(k)]
    left <- as.character(x) %in% left_levels

    wl <- sum(wt[left], na.rm = TRUE)
    wr <- sum(wt[!left], na.rm = TRUE)
    wtot <- wl + wr

    if (wl < ctrl$minbucket || wr < ctrl$minbucket) next
    if ((wl / wtot) < ctrl$minprob || (wr / wtot) < ctrl$minprob) next

    gains[k] <- weighted_ci_gain(y = y, wt = wt, left = left, ci_fun = ci_fun)
  }

  if (!any(is.finite(gains))) return(NULL)

  best_k <- which.max(gains)
  left_levels <- ord_levels[seq_len(best_k)]
  right_levels <- ord_levels[-seq_len(best_k)]

  index <- rep(NA_integer_, length(full_levels))
  index[match(left_levels,  full_levels)] <- 1L
  index[match(right_levels, full_levels)] <- 2L

  partykit::partysplit(
    varid = as.integer(varid),
    index = index
  )
}

```

```{r, example-best_factor_split}
x_full <- factor(
  c("low", "mid", "high", "low", "mid", "high"),
  levels = c("low", "mid", "high", "unused")
)
keep <- rep(TRUE, 6)
y_full <- cbind(
  ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  health = c(1, 2, 5, 1, 2, 5)
)
wt_full <- rep(1, 6)
ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

split <- best_factor_split(
  x_full = x_full,
  keep = keep,
  y_full = y_full,
  wt_full = wt_full,
  varid = 1,
  ctrl = ctrl,
  ci_fun = ci_factory("CI")
)
split$index
```

```{r, tests-best_factor_split}
test_that(
  "best_factor_split returns NULL when fewer than two levels are present",
  {
    x_full <- factor(c("low", "low", "mid"), levels = c("low", "mid", "high"))
    keep <- c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE)
    y_full <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30), health = c(1, 2, 3))
    wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
    ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

    expect_null(best_factor_split(
      x_full, keep, y_full, wt_full, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")
    ))
  }
)

test_that(
  "best_factor_split returns NULL when no admissible candidate split exists",
  {
    x_full <- factor(c("low", "mid", "high"), levels = c("low", "mid", "high"))
    keep <- rep(TRUE, 3)
    y_full <- cbind(ses = c(10, 20, 30), health = c(1, 2, 3))
    wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
    ctrl <- list(minbucket = 2, minprob = 0)

    expect_null(best_factor_split(
      x_full, keep, y_full, wt_full, 1, ctrl, ci_factory("CI")
    ))
  }
)

test_that("best_factor_split chooses the best cumulative level split", {
  x_full <- factor(
    c("high", "low", "mid"),
    levels = c("low", "mid", "high", "unused")
  )
  keep <- rep(TRUE, 3)
  y_full <- cbind(
    ses = c(30, 10, 20),
    health = c(10, 1, 2)
  )
  wt_full <- rep(1, 3)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_factor_split(
    x_full = x_full,
    keep = keep,
    y_full = y_full,
    wt_full = wt_full,
    varid = 4,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$varid, 4L)
  expect_equal(split$index, c(1L, 1L, 2L, NA_integer_))
})

test_that("best_factor_split respects minprob when scoring candidates", {
  x_full <- factor(
    c("low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"),
    levels = c("low", "mid", "high")
  )
  keep <- rep(TRUE, 5)
  y_full <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    health = c(1, 2, 2, 10, 10)
  )
  wt_full <- rep(1, 5)
  ctrl <- list(minbucket = 1, minprob = 0.4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)^2

  split <- best_factor_split(
    x_full = x_full,
    keep = keep,
    y_full = y_full,
    wt_full = wt_full,
    varid = 1,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = mock_ci_fun
  )

  expect_equal(split$index, c(1L, 1L, 2L))
})
```

```{r, function-ci_splitfun}
#' Create a concentration-index split function
#'
#' Build a split-selection function for inequality-aware tree fitting. The
#' returned function evaluates candidate predictors by the reduction in
#' within-node concentration index produced by each admissible split.
#'
#' The split function can recover the two-column response either from a matrix-
#' like response in the first model-frame column, or from separate columns named
#' by `rank_name` and `outcome_name`.
#'
#' @param rank_name Name of the socioeconomic rank variable in the model frame
#'   when the response is not supplied as a two-column matrix.
#' @param outcome_name Name of the outcome variable in the model frame when the
#'   response is not supplied as a two-column matrix.
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, or `"CIc"` selecting the concentration
#'   index variant used for split scoring.
#'
#' @return A function with signature
#'   `function(model, trafo, data, subset, weights, whichvar, ctrl)` suitable
#'   for tree-splitting workflows. It returns the first admissible `partysplit`
#'   found among `whichvar`, or `NULL` if no candidate variable yields a valid
#'   split.
#'
#' @export 

ci_splitfun <- function(rank_name, outcome_name, type = c("CI", "CIg", "CIc")) {
  type <- match.arg(type)
  ci_fun <- ci_factory(type)

  function(model, trafo, data, subset, weights, whichvar, ctrl) {
    mf <- stats::model.frame(data)

    if (is.null(weights) || length(weights) == 0L) {
      weights <- rep.int(1L, nrow(mf))
    }

    # For formulas like cbind(rank, outcome) ~ x1 + x2, the response may arrive
    # as a matrix-like object in the first model-frame column.
    response <- mf[[1L]]
    y_full <- NULL

    if (is.matrix(response) || is.data.frame(response)) {
      response_mat <- tryCatch(as.matrix(response), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (!is.null(response_mat) && is.matrix(response_mat) &&
          ncol(response_mat) >= 2L) {
        y_full <- response_mat[, 1:2, drop = FALSE]
      }
    }

    if (is.null(y_full)) {
      rank_vec <- mf[[rank_name]]
      outcome_vec <- mf[[outcome_name]]

      if (!is.null(rank_vec) && !is.null(outcome_vec)) {
        y_full <- cbind(rank = rank_vec, outcome = outcome_vec)
      }
    }

    if (is.null(y_full) || !is.matrix(y_full) || ncol(y_full) != 2L) {
      stop(
        "Unable to recover the two-column response expected by `ctree_ci()`. ",
        "Use a response like `cbind(rank, outcome) ~ ...`."
      )
    }

    colnames(y_full) <- c("rank", "outcome")

    # extree/.split may pass several candidate vars in ranked order
    for (j in whichvar) {
      x_full <- mf[[j]]

      keep <- subset[
        !is.na(x_full[subset]) &
        !is.na(y_full[subset, 1]) &
        !is.na(y_full[subset, 2]) &
        weights[subset] > 0
      ]

      if (!length(keep)) next

      w_node <- sum(weights[keep], na.rm = TRUE)
      if (w_node < ctrl$minsplit) next

      x_subset <- x_full[keep]

      sp <- NULL

      if (is.numeric(x_subset)) {
        sp <- best_numeric_split(
          x     = x_subset,
          y     = y_full[keep, , drop = FALSE],
          wt    = weights[keep],
          varid = j,
          ctrl  = ctrl,
          ci_fun = ci_fun
        )
      } else if (is.factor(x_subset) || is.ordered(x_subset)) {
        # use full x_full to preserve full level mapping in partysplit()
        sp <- best_factor_split(
          x_full = if (is.factor(x_full)) x_full else factor(x_full),
          keep   = keep,
          y_full = y_full,
          wt_full = weights,
          varid  = j,
          ctrl   = ctrl,
          ci_fun = ci_fun
        )
      }

      if (!is.null(sp)) return(sp)
    }

    NULL
  }
}
```

```{r, example-ci_splitfun}
splitfun <- ci_splitfun(
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  type = "CI"
)

toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10)
)

ctrl <- list(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

split <- splitfun(
  model = NULL,
  trafo = NULL,
  data = toy_data,
  subset = seq_len(nrow(toy_data)),
  weights = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
  whichvar = 3,
  ctrl = ctrl
)
split$breaks
```

```{r, tests-ci_splitfun}
test_that("ci_splitfun returns the same numeric split as best_numeric_split", {
  splitfun <- ci_splitfun("rank", "outcome", "CI")
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10)
  )
  ctrl <- list(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

  split <- splitfun(
    model = NULL,
    trafo = NULL,
    data = toy_data,
    subset = seq_len(nrow(toy_data)),
    weights = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    whichvar = 3,
    ctrl = ctrl
  )

  expected <- best_numeric_split(
    x = toy_data$income,
    y = as.matrix(toy_data[c("rank", "outcome")]),
    wt = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    varid = 3,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = ci_factory("CI")
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$breaks, expected$breaks)
  expect_equal(split$varid, expected$varid)
})

test_that("ci_splitfun returns the same factor split as best_factor_split", {
  splitfun <- ci_splitfun("rank", "outcome", "CI")
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    outcome = c(1, 2, 2, 10, 10),
    group = factor(c("low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )
  ctrl <- list(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

  split <- splitfun(
    model = NULL,
    trafo = NULL,
    data = toy_data,
    subset = seq_len(nrow(toy_data)),
    weights = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    whichvar = 3,
    ctrl = ctrl
  )

  expected <- best_factor_split(
    x_full = toy_data$group,
    keep = seq_len(nrow(toy_data)),
    y_full = as.matrix(toy_data[c("rank", "outcome")]),
    wt_full = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    varid = 3,
    ctrl = ctrl,
    ci_fun = ci_factory("CI")
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$index, expected$index)
  expect_equal(split$varid, expected$varid)
})

test_that(
  "ci_splitfun skips variables without admissible splits and tries next",
  {
  splitfun <- ci_splitfun("rank", "outcome", "CI")
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    outcome = c(1, 2, 2, 10, 10),
    constant = c(5, 5, 5, 5, 5),
    group = factor(c("low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )
  ctrl <- list(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

  split <- splitfun(
    model = NULL,
    trafo = NULL,
    data = toy_data,
    subset = seq_len(nrow(toy_data)),
    weights = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
    whichvar = c(3, 4),
    ctrl = ctrl
  )

  expect_s3_class(split, "partysplit")
  expect_equal(split$varid, 4L)
})

test_that("ci_splitfun errors when it cannot recover a two-column response", {
  splitfun <- ci_splitfun("rank", "outcome", "CI")
  toy_data <- data.frame(income = c(1, 2, 3), group = factor(c("a", "b", "a")))
  ctrl <- list(minsplit = 1, minbucket = 1, minprob = 0)

  expect_error(
    splitfun(
      model = NULL,
      trafo = NULL,
      data = toy_data,
      subset = seq_len(nrow(toy_data)),
      weights = rep(1, nrow(toy_data)),
      whichvar = 1:2,
      ctrl = ctrl
    ),
    "Unable to recover the two-column response"
  )
})
```


```{r, function-ctree_ci}
#' Fit a conditional inference tree using concentration-index split scoring
#'
#' Wrapper around [partykit::ctree()] that replaces the default split-selection
#' rule with a concentration-index-based split function created by
#' [ci_splitfun()].
#'
#' This helper is intended for inequality-aware tree fitting where the response
#' contains a socioeconomic ranking variable and a health outcome, and splits
#' are chosen to reduce within-node inequality rather than standard predictive
#' loss.
#'
#' @param formula A model formula, typically with a two-column response such as
#'   `cbind(rank, outcome) ~ x1 + x2`.
#' @param data A data frame containing the variables in `formula`.
#' @param rank_name Name of the socioeconomic rank variable.
#' @param outcome_name Name of the outcome variable.
#' @param weights Optional non-negative integer case weights. Non-integer
#'   weights must be converted before fitting because [partykit::ctree()]
#'   accepts integer case weights only.
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, or `"CIc"` selecting the concentration
#'   index variant used for split scoring.
#' @param control A control object created by [partykit::ctree_control()].
#' @param na.action A function for handling missing values.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [partykit::ctree()].
#'
#' @return A fitted `ci_tree` object inheriting from `party`.
#'
#' @export
ctree_ci <- function(formula,
                     data,
                     rank_name,
                     outcome_name,
                     weights = NULL,
                     type = c("CI", "CIg", "CIc"),
                     control = partykit::ctree_control(),
                     na.action = stats::na.omit,
                     ...) {
  type <- match.arg(type)

  if (!is.null(weights)) {
    if (any(weights < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
      stop("weights must be non-negative")
    }
    if (any(abs(weights - round(weights)) > 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {
      stop(
        "ctree() only accepts integer case weights. ",
        "Convert survey weights explicitly before fitting."
      )
    }
  }

  control$splitfun <- ci_splitfun(
    rank_name = rank_name,
    outcome_name = outcome_name,
    type = type
  )

  ctree_args <- list(
    formula = formula,
    data = data,
    control = control,
    na.action = na.action
  )

  if (!is.null(weights)) {
    ctree_args$weights <- as.integer(weights)
  }

  extra_args <- list(...)
  if (length(extra_args) > 0L) {
    ctree_args <- c(ctree_args, extra_args)
  }

  fit <- do.call(partykit::ctree, ctree_args)
  class(fit) <- unique(c("ci_tree", class(fit)))
  fit
}
```

```{r, example-ctree_ci}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- ctree_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
)

inherits(fit, "party")
```

```{r, tests-ctree_ci}
test_that("ctree_ci rejects negative weights", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8)
  )

  expect_error(
    ctree_ci(
      formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income,
      data = toy_data,
      rank_name = "rank",
      outcome_name = "outcome",
      weights = c(1, -1, 1, 1)
    ),
    "weights must be non-negative"
  )
})

test_that("ctree_ci rejects non-integer weights", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8)
  )

  expect_error(
    ctree_ci(
      formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income,
      data = toy_data,
      rank_name = "rank",
      outcome_name = "outcome",
      weights = c(1, 1.5, 1, 1)
    ),
    "integer case weights"
  )
})

test_that("ctree_ci fits a party object without explicit weights", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- ctree_ci(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_s3_class(fit, "party")
  expect_s3_class(fit, "ci_tree")
})

test_that("ctree_ci accepts integer weights", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12)
  )

  fit <- ctree_ci(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    weights = c(1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3),
    control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_s3_class(fit, "party")
  expect_s3_class(fit, "ci_tree")
})
```


```{r, function-as_caseweights}
#' Convert non-negative weights to integer case weights
#'
#' Rescale a non-negative weight vector so that its total is approximately
#' `scale`, then round to integers for use with fitting routines that require
#' integer case weights.
#'
#' @param w A numeric vector of non-negative weights.
#' @param scale Target total for the rescaled integer case weights.
#'
#' @return An integer vector of case weights on the same scale as `w`.
#'
#' @examples
#' as_caseweights(c(1, 2, 3), scale = 60L)
#' as_caseweights(c(0.2, 0.3, 0.5))
#'
#' @export

as_caseweights <- function(w, scale = 10000L) {
  stopifnot(all(w >= 0, na.rm = TRUE))
  if (!any(w > 0, na.rm = TRUE)) stop("all weights are zero")
  out <- round(w / sum(w, na.rm = TRUE) * scale)
  as.integer(out)
}
```

```{r, example-as_caseweights}
as_caseweights(c(1, 2, 3), scale = 60L)
as_caseweights(c(0.2, 0.3, 0.5))
```

```{r, tests-as_caseweights}
test_that("as_caseweights returns integer weights on the requested scale", {
  out <- as_caseweights(c(1, 2, 3), scale = 60L)

  expect_type(out, "integer")
  expect_equal(out, c(10L, 20L, 30L))
})

test_that("as_caseweights preserves weight proportions up to rounding", {
  out <- as_caseweights(c(0.2, 0.3, 0.5), scale = 100L)

  expect_equal(out, c(20L, 30L, 50L))
  expect_equal(sum(out), 100L)
})

test_that("as_caseweights rejects invalid input weights", {
  expect_error(as_caseweights(c(0, 0, 0)), "all weights are zero")
  expect_error(as_caseweights(c(1, -1, 2)))
})
```

```{r, function-cf_ci}
#' Fit a conditional inference forest using concentration-index split scoring
#'
#' Wrapper around [partykit::cforest()] that uses [ci_splitfun()] to score
#' candidate splits by reduction in within-node concentration index.
#'
#' This helper extends [ctree_ci()] to an ensemble setting, allowing
#' inequality-aware tree splits to be aggregated across many trees.
#'
#' @param formula A model formula, typically with a two-column response such as
#'   `cbind(rank, outcome) ~ x1 + x2`.
#' @param data A data frame containing the variables in `formula`.
#' @param rank_name Name of the socioeconomic rank variable.
#' @param outcome_name Name of the outcome variable.
#' @param weights Optional case weights passed to [partykit::cforest()].
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, or `"CIc"` selecting the concentration
#'   index variant used for split scoring.
#' @param control A control object, typically created by
#'   [partykit::ctree_control()].
#' @param ntree Number of trees to grow.
#' @param mtry Optional number of variables randomly tried at each split.
#' @param perturb Resampling specification passed to [partykit::cforest()].
#' @param na.action A function for handling missing values.
#' @param ... Additional arguments passed to [partykit::cforest()].
#'
#' @return A fitted forest object returned by [partykit::cforest()].
#'
#' nrow(stats::fitted(fit))
#'
#' @export

cf_ci <- function(formula,
                  data,
                  rank_name,
                  outcome_name,
                  weights = NULL,
                  type = c("CI", "CIg", "CIc"),
                  control = partykit::ctree_control(),
                  ntree = 500L,
                  mtry = NULL,
                  perturb = list(replace = FALSE, fraction = 0.632),
                  na.action = stats::na.omit,
                  ...) {

  type <- match.arg(type)

  control$splitfun <- ci_splitfun(
    rank_name = rank_name,
    outcome_name = outcome_name,
    type = type
  )

  cforest_args <- list(
    formula = formula,
    data = data,
    control = control,
    ntree = ntree,
    perturb = perturb,
    na.action = na.action
  )

  if (!is.null(weights)) {
    cforest_args$weights <- weights
  }

  extra_args <- list(...)
  if (length(extra_args) > 0L) {
    cforest_args <- c(cforest_args, extra_args)
  }

  if (!is.null(mtry)) {
    cforest_args$mtry <- mtry
  }

  do.call(partykit::cforest, cforest_args)
}
```

```{r, example-cf_ci}
toy_data <- data.frame(
  rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
  income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
  group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
)

fit <- cf_ci(
  formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
  data = toy_data,
  rank_name = "rank",
  outcome_name = "outcome",
  ntree = 10L,
  mtry = 1L,
  control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
)

nrow(stats::fitted(fit))
```

```{r, tests-cf_ci}
test_that(
  "cf_ci fits a forest and stores one fitted row per observation", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- cf_ci(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    ntree = 10L,
    mtry = 1L,
    control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_s3_class(fit, "cforest")
  expect_equal(nrow(stats::fitted(fit)), nrow(toy_data))
})

test_that("cf_ci accepts explicit weights", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12)
  )

  fit <- cf_ci(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    weights = c(1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3),
    ntree = 10L,
    control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_s3_class(fit, "cforest")
  expect_equal(nrow(stats::fitted(fit)), nrow(toy_data))
})

test_that("cf_ci honors mtry when supplied", {
  toy_data <- data.frame(
    rank = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    outcome = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1),
    income = c(2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12),
    group = factor(c("low", "low", "mid", "mid", "high", "high"))
  )

  fit <- cf_ci(
    formula = cbind(rank, outcome) ~ income + group,
    data = toy_data,
    rank_name = "rank",
    outcome_name = "outcome",
    ntree = 10L,
    mtry = 1L,
    control = partykit::ctree_control(mincriterion = 0, maxdepth = 1)
  )

  expect_equal(fit$info$control$mtry, 1)
})
```



```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(
  flat_file = "dev/flat_partytrees.Rmd",
  vignette_name = "Inequality-aware party trees"
)
```
